# Changes in version 1.2.1.9000-7 (2026-04-21)

## Breaking changes

- In the function `fit_DoseReponseCurve()` we modified the approach of
  how weights are considered for the fitting. In the past, we had used a
  normalised inverse standard error approach. This was under the
  assumption that the uncertainties somewhat scale with the problem.
  However, in hindsight this appears to be short-sighted and not very
  well justified. Individual relative uncertainties should not scale
  with the dose, but are independent unless in exceptional cases. We
  modified the allowed input to accommodate the old and new approach on
  top of an option to provide own weights. This has three immediate
  consequences:

1)  The allowed input for `fit.weights` changes from `logical` to
    `character` and `numeric` plus `NULL` (no weights); (2) the
    numerical output of `fit_DoseReponseCurve()` will differ, as the new
    default will use inverse variance instead; (3) users may provide
    their own weights. These changes affect the numerical output of the
    following functions: `fit_DoseResponseCurve()`,
    `analyse_SAR.CWOSL()`, `analyse_SAR.TL()`, `analyse_baSAR()`,
    `analyse_pIRIRSequence()`, `calc_Lamothe2003()`,
    `calc_Huntley2006()`

Although the change in the numerical output may be inconvenient, we
strongly advise updating regardless. The old results can still be
reproduced using the option `fit.weights = "norm_inverse_std"` in the
affected functions.

## Removed functions and deprecations

## Bugfixes and changes

### `analyse_SAR.CWOSL()`

- The default value for the `background_integral` argument has changed
  from `NA` to `NULL`. In v1.2.0 `NA` acquired the distinct meaning “do
  not subtract the background integral”, so using it as the implicit
  default changed user-facing behaviour. Switching the default to
  `NULL`restores the behaviour present up to v1.1.2, where integrals are
  applied only when both signal and background integrals are provided,
  and specifying only the signal integral results in no integrals being
  applied (#1526).

### `calc_MinDose()`

- If `init.values` is provided and `log = TRUE`, the `sigma` parameter
  is now initialised as `sigma / mu` instead of `log(sigma)`. The
  previous approach failed silently when `sigma < 1`, as the logarithm
  produced out-of-bounds parameter values, with the effect of leaving
  all parameters at their initial values (#1527; thanks to
  @MarijnvanderMeij for reporting and suggesting a fix).

- The confidence intervals stored in the `RLum.Results` object are now
  always in the natural units, independently of the `log` argument
  (#1533; thanks to @MarijnvanderMeij for reporting).

### `convert_Second2Gray()`

- Input validation was strengthened to avoid two possible crashes
  (#1537).

### `fit_DoseResponseCurve()`

- For `fit.method = "OTORX"` parameter `a` was renamed to `Di` for
  consistency.

- The argument `fit.weights` now supports a `numeric` vector as input.
  This gives more control over the fitting process and enables better
  comparison with other software, such as Python’s SciPy. The `numeric`
  vector must be of the same length as the number of data points to be
  fit. This is typical the number of `LxTx` points (#1536)

### `plot_DoseResponseCurve()`

- The error bars had been missing for `extrapolation` and `alternate`
  mode. This error was introduced with commit \#cf299831 and remained
  unnoticed due to incorrect graphical snapshots.

### `remove_RLum()`

- The function no longer accepts unnamed arguments (#1529).
